arguments.prompt:
Review all MSBuild project files (.csproj, .props, .targets, .sln) in the repository at /tmp/sv-379cb7f49bca4f2a8c9fc569b46522eb for anti-patterns, duplication, modernization opportunities, and best practices violations. 

The solution has "grown organically" so pay special attention to:
1. Duplicated properties/settings across project files that should be centralized (e.g., into Directory.Build.props/targets)
2. Package version management - scattered versions vs central package management
3. SDK-style project modernization opportunities
4. Common MSBuild anti-patterns (hardcoded paths, restated defaults, unquoted conditions, etc.)
5. Build organization improvements for maintainability

Provide actionable, concrete suggestions with BAD → GOOD transformations where applicable. Group findings by category (duplication, anti-patterns, modernization, etc.).

  <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
  <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>

  <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
</PropertyGroup>

```xml

    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>

Both test projects reference the same 4 test packages. If you expect to add more test projects, consider a `test/Directory.Build.props` that auto-includes the test infrastructure:
